enrichment.short_description: Teamtailor is a Swedish ATS and employer branding platform combining recruitment workflows with a drag-and-drop career site builder and AI tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Teamtailor is a Swedish applicant tracking system (ATS) and employer branding platform founded in 2013 and headquartered in Stockholm. It was built as a "next generation" alternative to older, workflow-only recruiting software, pairing the core mechanics of an ATS with tools for building a polished, on-brand careers site. The company now serves organizations across dozens of countries, ranging from small scale-ups to large enterprises with thousands of employees.

The product's defining idea is that recruiting is as much a marketing problem as an operations problem. Rather than treating the careers page as an afterthought bolted onto a job board, Teamtailor gives recruiting and people teams a visual, drag-and-drop site editor so they can present culture, benefits, and open roles without needing a developer. That career site sits on top of a full pipeline, interview, and collaboration toolset, so hiring managers and recruiters work from a shared, searchable database of candidates rather than a maze of spreadsheets and email threads.

More recently, Teamtailor has layered an AI Co-pilot across the platform, adding candidate matching against talent pools, evaluation against hiring criteria, interview question suggestions, CV summarization, and AI-assisted job ad writing and translation. Combined with a large marketplace of integrations for job boards, background checks, e-signing, and HR systems, the platform aims to be a single hub that covers sourcing, screening, employer branding, and onboarding.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Teamtailor Is For
paragraphs: Teamtailor is aimed squarely at growing companies that treat hiring as a brand-building exercise, not just a back-office task. Startups and scale-ups that are hiring across multiple markets tend to be a strong fit, since the tool was built to make a small people team look and operate like a much larger one. Recruiting and talent acquisition teams without in-house developers or designers benefit most, because the career site builder removes the need for custom web work to keep the jobs page current and on-brand.

Companies with distributed or international hiring also fit well, thanks to built-in localization for both the internal interface and candidate-facing career sites, along with AI-assisted translation of job content. Organizations that care about structured, collaborative hiring decisions - shared scorecards, interview kits, automated candidate communication - will find the workflow tooling maps closely to modern hiring best practices rather than replicating a legacy paper trail.

It also suits companies that expect to scale their hiring volume over time, since pricing and account structure are built around concurrent open roles and company size tiers rather than a fixed per-seat model that gets expensive as teams add more hiring managers and interviewers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Should Avoid It
paragraphs: Teamtailor is a weaker match for solo founders, very small businesses, or companies that hire only occasionally, since the platform is sold on annual contracts rather than flexible month-to-month or pay-as-you-go terms, and pricing is quote-based rather than published, which makes it hard to test at a low commitment level. Organizations that need to keep costs minimal and predictable from day one may prefer a lighter, self-serve ATS instead.

Enterprises with deep, highly specific compliance or reporting requirements - for example, heavy OFCCP or EEOC reporting obligations in regulated US hiring, or tight integration with a specific legacy HRIS - should validate those workflows carefully in a demo before committing, since Teamtailor's roots and strongest customer base are in Europe and general commercial hiring rather than US government-contractor compliance. Companies that already have a heavily customized HR tech stack with rigid integration requirements may also find the switching effort nontrivial.

Finally, teams that specifically want native mobile apps for recruiters and hiring managers should be aware that Teamtailor operates as a responsive web application rather than dedicated iOS or Android apps, which can matter for interviewers who lean on a phone for on-the-go candidate reviews.

long_form_blocks.heading: Main Features
paragraphs: At the center of Teamtailor is its career site builder, a drag-and-drop editor with pre-built content blocks - job listings, team and culture pages, video headers, testimonials, and custom pages - that lets non-technical teams ship a professional careers site without a developer, and localize it into multiple languages for different markets. Around that sits the core ATS: customizable pipelines, interview kits and scorecards, and a searchable talent pool so past applicants can be revisited for future openings.

The AI Co-pilot adds candidate matching and ranking against a role's requirements, AI-assisted job ad and question writing, CV summarization, and translation support for multilingual hiring. Automation triggers handle repetitive steps - moving candidates through stages, sending scheduled emails, or notifying hiring managers - while built-in analytics and reporting surface pipeline health, source performance, and time-to-hire metrics.

Structured onboarding workflows extend the platform past the offer stage, and a large marketplace of integrations connects job boards, background-check providers, e-signature tools, and broader HR systems into the same workspace.

long_form_blocks.heading: The Teamtailor Experience
paragraphs: Day to day, Teamtailor is consistently described as clean, visual, and easy to pick up, which is a notable contrast with older ATS interfaces that require significant training. Recruiters and hiring managers generally report a short ramp-up time, and candidate-facing career sites are frequently singled out as a strength that helps smaller employer brands look more established.

Support is a recurring positive in customer feedback, with users citing responsive help during setup and ongoing use. The tradeoff some teams report is friction at the edges: customization can feel limited outside the main career site builder, calendar syncing occasionally misbehaves, and a handful of automated notifications - like emails to existing candidates when a new role is posted - are not fully configurable.

Overall, the experience favors teams that want a modern, marketing-forward front end paired with a capable but not maximally configurable backend ATS. Companies coming from spreadsheets or a bare-bones job board typically see the biggest jump in perceived professionalism and hiring team efficiency, while teams migrating from highly customized enterprise ATS platforms may notice a narrower, more opinionated feature set.

features.title: Drag-and-drop career site builder
features.description: A visual editor with pre-built blocks for job listings, culture pages, video headers, and testimonials, letting non-technical teams ship a branded careers site without a developer.

features.title: Applicant tracking pipelines
features.description: Customizable hiring stages, structured interview kits, and shared scorecards so hiring teams collaborate on the same candidate data instead of email threads.

features.title: AI Co-pilot
features.description: AI-assisted job ad writing, candidate matching against talent pools, evaluation against hiring criteria, interview question suggestions, and CV summarization.

features.title: Talent pool and CRM
features.description: A searchable database of past applicants and passive candidates that recruiters can revisit and nurture for future openings.

features.title: Automation and triggers
features.description: Rule-based automations that move candidates between stages, send scheduled emails, or alert hiring managers without manual follow-up.

features.title: Multilingual career sites and localization
features.description: Interface and career-site content can be localized into multiple languages, with AI-assisted translation of job content for global hiring.

features.title: Analytics and reporting
features.description: Dashboards covering pipeline health, source performance, and time-to-hire to help teams see where hiring is slowing down.

features.title: Onboarding tools
features.description: Workflows that extend past the offer stage to help new hires get set up and integrated with the company.

features.title: Integrations marketplace
features.description: Connections to job boards, background-check providers, e-signature tools, assessments, and broader HR tech stacks.

pros: Modern, visually polished career site builder that doesn't require developer help
Intuitive interface with a short learning curve for recruiters and hiring managers
Responsive customer support frequently praised in user reviews
AI Co-pilot adds practical time savings for job ad writing, matching, and translation
Unlimited users and job postings included rather than per-seat pricing
cons: Pricing is quote-based and not published, making it hard to compare costs upfront
Sold on annual contracts with no month-to-month or pay-as-you-go option
Customization options outside the career site builder can feel limited
No dedicated native mobile apps, only a responsive web experience
Some automated notifications, like emails to existing candidates on new postings, aren't fully configurable

faqs.question: What is Teamtailor used for?
faqs.answer: Teamtailor is an applicant tracking system and employer branding platform used to manage recruitment pipelines, build a branded careers site, and collaborate on hiring decisions in one place.

faqs.question: Does Teamtailor offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Teamtailor is commonly offered with a free trial period so teams can evaluate the platform before committing to an annual contract; exact trial terms are best confirmed directly with Teamtailor since they can change.

faqs.question: How is Teamtailor priced?
faqs.answer: Teamtailor uses custom, quote-based pricing tied to company size and the number of concurrent open roles rather than a fixed per-seat fee, and typically requires an annual contract.

faqs.question: Does Teamtailor have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Teamtailor does not offer dedicated native iOS or Android apps; it is designed as a responsive web application that works on mobile browsers.

faqs.question: What languages does Teamtailor support?
faqs.answer: Teamtailor's interface and career sites can be localized into multiple languages, including English, Swedish, Danish, German, Spanish, Finnish, French, Italian, Dutch, Norwegian, and Portuguese (Brazilian), among others.

use_cases.title: Building an employer brand without developer resources
use_cases.description: A people team uses the drag-and-drop career site builder to launch and maintain a professional, on-brand jobs page featuring culture content and testimonials, without needing engineering support.
use_cases.audience: HR and talent acquisition teams at growing companies

use_cases.title: Running structured, collaborative hiring pipelines
use_cases.description: Hiring managers and recruiters use shared interview kits and scorecards to evaluate candidates consistently and move them through pipeline stages together.

use_cases.title: Hiring across multiple countries and languages
use_cases.description: An international company localizes its career site and internal interface into several languages and uses AI-assisted translation for job postings to reach candidates in different markets.

use_cases.title: Automating repetitive recruiting admin work
use_cases.description: A talent team sets up triggers to automatically move candidates, send status emails, and notify stakeholders, reducing manual follow-up during high-volume hiring periods.
use_cases.audience: Recruiting operations teams handling high applicant volume

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Teamtailor combines an ATS, drag-and-drop career site builder, and AI co-pilot in one employer branding platform. See features, pricing plans, and reviews.

enrichment.short_description: Teamwork.com is an AI-powered PSA platform for agencies and client-services teams to manage projects, resources, budgets, and profitability.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Teamwork.com is a project and client-work management platform founded in 2007 in Cork, Ireland, where it remains headquartered. It began as an internal tool built by a web design agency that needed better visibility into client projects, deadlines, and billable hours, and it has since grown into a company serving thousands of agencies and professional-services teams worldwide. Today Teamwork.com markets itself as "AI-powered PSA software built for profitability," reflecting a shift from a general task-tracking tool toward a fuller professional services automation suite.

The platform combines classic project management building blocks, tasks, boards, lists, Gantt charts, and milestones, with features aimed squarely at businesses that bill clients for their time. Time tracking, budgets, invoicing, resource scheduling, and profitability reporting sit alongside the project views, so a single workspace can show whether a project is on schedule and whether it is still making money. Client-facing features like portals and permission controls let agencies collaborate with customers without exposing internal chatter or financial detail.

Teamwork.com competes with a broad field of project and work management tools, from Asana and Monday.com to PSA-focused products like Kantata and Productive. Its differentiator is the tight coupling of project delivery with the financial side of client services work: budgets, retainers, and utilization reporting are treated as first-class citizens rather than bolt-ons. Recent additions of TeamworkAI and AI-driven automation continue that trajectory, aiming to help teams plan capacity and flag at-risk budgets earlier.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Teamwork Is For
paragraphs: Teamwork.com is built primarily for agencies and professional services businesses that bill clients by the hour or by project, including marketing and creative agencies, software and IT consultancies, architecture and engineering firms, and accounting or business-advisory practices. These teams typically juggle several client projects at once and need one place to see delivery status, team capacity, and profitability together.

It also suits operations and project leads at small to mid-sized companies (roughly 10 to a few hundred people) who need more structure than a simple task list but do not want the overhead of enterprise software. Teams that already track billable hours, negotiate retainers, or produce client invoices tend to get the most value, since those workflows map directly onto Teamwork.com's budgeting and time-tracking features.

Client-facing teams that want to give customers limited visibility into project status, without granting access to internal comments or margins, are another strong fit. The client-permission model and portal features were clearly designed with agency-client relationships in mind, which is a less common emphasis among general-purpose project management tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Should Avoid It
paragraphs: Solo freelancers and very small teams with simple to-do lists rather than multi-stage client projects will likely find Teamwork.com more tool than they need; a lighter product such as Trello or a basic task app will feel faster to set up and cheaper to run. The free plan caps users, projects, and storage tightly enough that it functions more as a trial than a long-term free option for growing teams.

Organizations that do not bill clients for time, internal-only teams, product companies without external client work, or nonprofits, may not need the budgeting, invoicing, and profitability features that define Teamwork.com's positioning, and could get equivalent task management elsewhere for less money and less setup effort.

Software engineering teams looking for deep sprint planning, backlog grooming, and issue-tracking depth comparable to Jira or Linear should also look elsewhere, since Teamwork.com's task and board views are general-purpose rather than built around agile development ceremonies. Likewise, teams wanting the most affordable entry point at scale may find per-user pricing on the higher tiers adds up quickly once dozens of seats are involved.

long_form_blocks.heading: Main Features
paragraphs: Core project management covers list, board (kanban), Gantt chart, and table views of tasks, along with milestones, task dependencies, and reusable project templates so recurring client work can be spun up quickly. Built-in time tracking lets team members log billable and non-billable hours directly against tasks, feeding into reporting and invoicing rather than living in a separate timesheet tool.

Resource scheduling and capacity planning show who is over- or under-allocated across projects, while budgeting tools track estimated versus actual costs and flag projects at risk of going over budget. Client management features include portals and permission settings so external stakeholders can view progress without seeing internal discussion, plus invoicing and integrations with accounting tools like QuickBooks.

Automation rules reduce repetitive status updates and task assignments, and smart forms can turn incoming work requests into structured tasks. TeamworkAI adds AI-assisted features such as summarization and suggested next steps, and the platform connects to 150-plus third-party tools, including HubSpot, Salesforce, Slack, and Google Workspace, so it can sit inside an existing operational stack rather than replacing it.

long_form_blocks.heading: The Teamwork Experience
paragraphs: New users generally describe Teamwork.com's interface as more detailed than lightweight task managers, which brings real power for budgeting and resourcing but also a steeper initial learning curve. Getting full value out of the platform, particularly the financial reporting, usually means spending real time on setup: defining project templates, budget types, and permission structures before rolling it out team-wide.

The product is available as a web application with companion iOS and Android mobile apps for logging time and checking task status on the go. Support options scale with plan tier, from self-serve help docs and community resources on lower tiers to a dedicated customer success manager and priority support on enterprise-level plans, reflecting the product's lean toward mid-market and larger client-services teams.

Because Teamwork.com started life as an agency's internal tool, its day-to-day feel still favors teams managing multiple concurrent client engagements over teams running a single internal roadmap. Migration paths and templates for agencies switching from spreadsheets, email, or simpler task tools are a recurring theme in its own marketing and support content, underscoring who the product is ultimately built to serve.

features.title: Multiple Project Views
features.description: Switch between list, board (kanban), Gantt chart, and table views to plan and track tasks in the format that fits the team or project.

features.title: Time Tracking
features.description: Log billable and non-billable hours directly against tasks, feeding automatically into reporting, budgets, and client invoices.

features.title: Resource Scheduling
features.description: See who is over- or under-allocated across active projects to balance workloads and plan capacity before it becomes a bottleneck.

features.title: Budgets and Financial Reporting
features.description: Track estimated versus actual project costs, monitor profitability, and get alerts when a project is at risk of going over budget.

features.title: Client Portals and Permissions
features.description: Give clients limited, controlled visibility into project progress without exposing internal comments, files, or financial detail.

features.title: Automation and Smart Forms
features.description: Automate repetitive status updates and task assignments, and convert incoming work requests into structured tasks via forms.

features.title: Invoicing and Accounting Integrations
features.description: Generate client invoices from tracked time and connect to accounting tools such as QuickBooks to streamline billing.

features.title: TeamworkAI
features.description: AI-assisted features that help summarize project activity, draft updates, and surface suggested next steps within projects.

features.title: Project Templates
features.description: Reusable templates for recurring client engagements speed up project setup and keep delivery processes consistent.

features.title: Third-Party Integrations
features.description: Connect with 150-plus tools, including HubSpot, Salesforce, Slack, and Google Workspace, to fit into an existing operational stack.

pros: Combines project delivery with time tracking, budgeting, and profitability reporting in one workspace
Client portals and permission controls are well suited to agency-client relationships
Resource scheduling and capacity planning help balance workloads across multiple projects
Deep integration ecosystem, including accounting tools like QuickBooks, for client-services workflows
Mobile apps for iOS and Android let teams log time and check status away from a desk
cons: Steeper learning curve than lightweight task managers due to the breadth of features
Free plan is tightly capped on users, projects, and storage, limiting long-term use for growing teams
Per-user pricing on higher tiers can become expensive as teams scale past a handful of seats
Advanced financial and resourcing features, custom reports, and SSO are reserved for higher-priced or custom-quoted plans
Less suited to software development teams needing deep agile/sprint tooling than dedicated dev-focused tools

faqs.question: What is Teamwork.com used for?
faqs.answer: Teamwork.com is a project management and professional services automation (PSA) platform used mainly by agencies and client-services businesses to plan projects, track billable time, manage resources and budgets, and report on profitability.

faqs.question: Does Teamwork.com have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Teamwork.com offers a free plan limited to a small number of users, projects, and storage, alongside paid plans that unlock higher limits and more advanced features, with a 30-day free trial available on paid tiers.

faqs.question: How much does Teamwork.com cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at $9.99 per user per month when billed annually for the entry-level tier, with higher tiers priced per user per month and the top enterprise tiers requiring a custom quote from sales.

faqs.question: Is Teamwork.com good for agencies?
faqs.answer: Teamwork.com is built with agencies in mind, offering client portals, budgeting, retainer tracking, and billable time reporting alongside standard project management features, which is why it is popular with marketing, IT, and consulting agencies.

faqs.question: Does Teamwork.com have mobile apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, Teamwork.com provides mobile apps for iOS and Android so team members can view tasks, update status, and log time from a phone or tablet.

use_cases.title: Managing Multiple Client Projects
use_cases.description: Agencies running several concurrent client engagements use Teamwork.com to track deadlines, deliverables, and ownership across projects in one workspace.

use_cases.title: Tracking Billable Time and Profitability
use_cases.description: Teams log billable and non-billable hours against tasks and compare estimated versus actual costs to see whether a project is staying profitable.

use_cases.title: Balancing Team Capacity
use_cases.description: Resource scheduling views help operations leads spot over-allocated staff and redistribute work before deadlines slip.

use_cases.title: Collaborating With External Clients
use_cases.description: Client portals give customers visibility into project progress and approvals without exposing internal discussion or budgets.

features: Task, list, board, and Gantt views
Team capacity planning and time budgets
Resource scheduling by role or skill
Advanced security, SSO, and dedicated success manager

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Teamwork.com: AI-powered PSA software for agencies to manage projects, time, budgets, resources, and client work. Free plan available.
